zh.Berkel, Brabant, Nederland: 荷蘭努-布拉班特貝可爾
zh.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made是IJsbrand van der Made的女兒，於1200年出生在荷蘭。她於大約1224年與Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet在荷蘭布拉班特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.Berkel, Brabant, Nederland: 荷兰努-布拉班特貝可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made是IJsbrand van der Made的女儿，于1200年出生在荷兰。她于大约1224年与Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet在荷兰布拉班特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

(iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Made
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a variant of the personal name Matthäus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Ada
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin; it was not in general use before the late 18th century. It may be a Latinate variant of the biblical name Adah . However, it has also been explained as a pet form of Adele and Adelaide , Germanic female names of which the first element is adal ‘noble’. It was borne by a 7th-century abbess of Saint-Julien-des-Prés at Le Mans.

en.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made was born in 1200, in Netherlands as the daughter of IJsbrand van der Made. She married Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet about 1224, in Brabant, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made est née en 1200, à Pays-Bas, fille de IJsbrand van der Made. Elle a épousé Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet environ 1224, à Brabant, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made wurde 1200 in Niederlande, als Tochter von IJsbrand van der Made geboren. Sie hat um 1224 in Brabant, Niederlande Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made è nata il nel 1200 nel Paesi Bassi come figlia di IJsbrand van der Made. Lei sposato Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet circa 1224, nel Brabant, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Berkel, Brabant, Nederland: オランダノオルド-ブラバントバーケル
ja.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Madeは1200年にオランダでIJsbrand van der Madeの娘として生まれた。約1224年にオランダブラバントでHeer Willem Jan van Wijtvlietと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made(은)는 1200년 네덜란드에서 IJsbrand van der Made의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1224년 네덜란드 브라반트에서 Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.Berkel, Brabant, Nederland: Berkel, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made nasceu em 1200, em Países Baixos filha de IJsbrand van der Made. Ela casou-se com Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet aproximadamente 1224, em Brabant, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made родилась в 1200 г. в местности Нидерланды. Её отца звали IJsbrand van der Made. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet около 1224 г. в местности брабант, Нидерланды. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.Berkel, Brabant, Nederland: Berkel, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Dame Ada Ijsbrandsdr van Wijtvliet van der Made nació en 1200, en Países Bajos como hija de IJsbrand van der Made. Se casó con Heer Willem Jan van Wijtvliet aproximadamente 1224, en Brabant, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo.
